varieties.name: Mysore Type 🌱
varieties.origin: Karnataka, India (native variety, 100+ years)
varieties.traits: Robust plants 3-4m tall, erect panicles grow vertically upwards, smooth glabrous leaves on both sides, dark green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: 375-450 kg dry capsules/hectare
varieties.availability: Available through Queensland specialist herb nurseries and online retailers

varieties.name: Vazhukka 🧬
varieties.origin: Kerala/Tamil Nadu - Natural hybrid between Malabar and Mysore types
varieties.traits: Medium height plants, semi-erect panicles (between horizontal and vertical), deep green oblong-lanceolate leaves, compact growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good yields, better than both parent varieties in optimal conditions
varieties.availability: Limited availability in Australia, may need to import seeds

varieties.name: Green Gold / Njallani
varieties.origin: Kattappana, Kerala, India - Developed by farmer Sebastian Joseph and son Regimon (1990s)
varieties.traits: High-yielding farmer-bred variety, requires 40% less shade than traditional varieties, year-round harvesting potential, deep green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: 1500 kg/hectare (vs conventional 200 kg/ha) - Revolutionary high yield

varieties.name: ICRI-1 (Malabar)
varieties.origin: Indian Cardamom Research Institute (ICRI), Myladumpara, Kerala - Released 1980s
varieties.traits: Early maturing variety, profuse flowering, medium-sized panicle, globose extra bold dark green capsules
varieties.yield_notes: 656 kg dry capsules/hectare

varieties.name: ICRI-2 (Mysore)
varieties.origin: ICRI, Myladumpara, Kerala - Released 1980s
varieties.traits: Performs well under irrigated conditions, suitable for higher altitudes, medium-long panicles, tolerant to azhukal disease
varieties.yield_notes: 766 kg dry capsules/hectare

varieties.name: Malabar (Nadan/Native) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Western Ghats, Kerala - Native to Malabar Coast (100+ years, indigenous)
varieties.traits: Plants under 3m tall, prostrate panicles grow horizontally along ground, hairy underside of leaves, compact growth
varieties.yield_notes: 260-500 kg dry capsules/ha, stable traditional yields
varieties.availability: Kerala Spices Board nurseries, KAU Cardamom Research Station Pampadumpara, private Idukki growers, Graamya Organic Spices

varieties.name: Mysore 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Karnataka (Mysore region) - Indigenous to Western Ghats of Karnataka (100+ years)
varieties.traits: Robust plants over 3m tall, completely erect panicles grow vertically upward, smooth glabrous leaves, dark green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: 375-450 kg dry capsules/ha
varieties.availability: Karnataka State Spices Development Board, ICRI Regional Research Station Sakleshpur

varieties.name: Vazhukka (Vazuka) 🏆 🧬 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala/Tamil Nadu - Natural hybrid between Malabar and Mysore (70+ years)
varieties.traits: Semi-erect panicles (intermediate between parents), short plant height, deep green oblong-lanceolate leaves, consistent robust shape
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields exceeding both parent varieties
varieties.availability: Widely available - Kerala Spices Board, ICRI Myladumpara, KAU Pampadumpara, local Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Njallani (Green Gold)
varieties.origin: Kattappana, Idukki, Kerala - Developed by farmer Sebastian Joseph and son Regimon (1990s)
varieties.traits: Requires 40% less shade than traditional varieties, year-round harvesting capability, robust growth, multiple productive suckers
varieties.yield_notes: 1500 kg/ha (revolutionary 7.5x increase over conventional 200 kg/ha), shortened yield span to 2 years instead of 3

varieties.name: Thiruthali (Thirumali)
varieties.origin: Santhanpara, Idukki, Kerala - Developed by farmer T.P. Joseph (2000s)
varieties.traits: Malabar type, fibrous stem resistant to stem borers, low maintenance requirements, high resistance to pests and diseases, less fertilizer needs
varieties.yield_notes: 4-6 kg dried cardamom per plant (exceptionally high), 2000-3000 kg/acre dry weight, 22% curing weight
varieties.availability: T.P. Joseph's nursery (Santhampara, Idukki), Kerala agricultural institutions, distributed through Spices Board

varieties.name: ICRI-1 (Malabar)
varieties.origin: ICRI Myladumpara, Kerala - Released 1980s for Kerala Zone
varieties.traits: Malabar type, early maturing variety, profuse flowering, medium-sized panicle
varieties.yield_notes: 656 kg/ha dry capsules
varieties.availability: ICRI Myladumpara, Spices Board nurseries, Kerala Spices Board outlets

varieties.name: ICRI-2 (Mysore)
varieties.origin: ICRI Myladumpara - Released 1980s for Kerala Zone
varieties.traits: Mysore type clonal selection, erect type, high yielder, suitable for higher altitudes, performs well under irrigation, medium-long panicles, tolerant to azhukal disease
varieties.yield_notes: 766 kg/ha dry capsules (highest among ICRI varieties)

varieties.name: ICRI-3
varieties.origin: ICRI RRS Sakleshpur, Karnataka - Released for Karnataka Zone
varieties.traits: Two high-yielding Malabar type selections, developed at Regional Research Station Donigal/Mudigere
varieties.availability: ICRI RRS Sakleshpur, Karnataka State Spices Development Board

varieties.name: ICRI-4
varieties.origin: ICRI RRS Thadiyankudisai - Released for Tamil Nadu Zone
varieties.traits: Relative resistance to rhizome rot disease
varieties.availability: ICRI RRS Thadiyankudisai (Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu)

varieties.name: ICRI-5
varieties.origin: ICRI Myladumpara - Released for Kerala Zone
varieties.traits: Selection from Chakkupallam collections (MCC-49)
varieties.yield_notes: Good commercial yields

varieties.name: ICRI-6
varieties.origin: ICRI Myladumpara - Released for Kerala Zone

varieties.name: ICRI-7
varieties.origin: ICRI Myladumpara - Released for Kerala Zone

varieties.name: ICRI-8
varieties.origin: ICRI RRS Sakleshpur - Released for Karnataka Zone
varieties.traits: High-yielding Malabar type selection

varieties.name: PV-1
varieties.origin: KAU Cardamom Research Station, Pampadumpara - Released 1970s-1980s
varieties.traits: Malabar type, pure line selection from Walayar collection, plant height 269cm, 39 tillers per clump, 26 panicles per clump, 79 flowers per panicle
varieties.yield_notes: 260 kg/ha average (potential 500 kg/ha), 1310g green capsules per clump, 19.9% dry recovery, capsules mature in 110 days
varieties.availability: KAU Cardamom Research Station Pampadumpara, RARS Ambalavayal

varieties.name: PV-2
varieties.origin: KAU Cardamom Research Station, Pampadumpara - Released 2000s
varieties.traits: Selection from open-pollinated seedlings of PV-1, developed for bold capsules, suitable for Cardamom Hill Reserves
varieties.yield_notes: 982 kg/ha (highest among KAU varieties), 23.8% capsule recovery

varieties.name: PV-3
varieties.origin: KAU Cardamom Research Station, Pampadumpara
varieties.traits: Early maturing variety
varieties.yield_notes: 380 kg/ha

varieties.name: IISR Vijetha-1
varieties.origin: ICAR-IISR Calicut - Released 2001
varieties.traits: Malabar type, green aerial shoots, plant height 172cm, 36 tillers per clump, field tolerance to thrips and shoot/panicle/capsule borers
varieties.yield_notes: 643 kg/ha dry capsules (potential 979 kg/ha)
varieties.growing_notes: Strong resistance to Katte disease (Cardamom Mosaic Virus - CMV). This is the key distinguishing feature.

varieties.name: IISR Avinash (RR-1)
varieties.origin: ICAR-IISR Cardamom Research Centre, Appangala, Karnataka - Released 2006
varieties.traits: Derived from progeny of CCS-1, clonal selection, extended flowering period, ideal for rhizome rot-infested areas (valleys)
varieties.yield_notes: 643 kg/ha average (potential 979 kg/ha), widely appreciated for high productivity
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to rhizome rot disease (Pythium vexans, Rhizoctonia solani). Critical resistance trait.

varieties.name: IISR Suvarna
varieties.origin: ICAR-IISR Calicut
varieties.traits: IISR-released high-yielding selection

varieties.name: Mudigere-1
varieties.origin: UAS Regional Research Station, Mudigere, Karnataka - Released 1984
varieties.traits: Malabar type clonal selection, pubescent type (hairy), relatively low genetic variability (stable type)
varieties.yield_notes: 250-300 kg/ha, 1.2 kg green capsule per clump
varieties.availability: UAS Mudigere, Karnataka State Spices Development Board

varieties.name: Mudigere-2
varieties.origin: UAS Regional Research Station, Mudigere - Released 1994
varieties.traits: Malabar type clonal selection, non-pubescent leaves, panicles not compact, good branching
varieties.yield_notes: 475 kg/ha average, 1.4 kg green capsule per clump (improved over M-1)

varieties.name: Mudigere-3
varieties.origin: UAS Mudigere - Later release
varieties.traits: Malabar type selection

varieties.name: CCS-1 (Coorg Cardamom Selection-1 / Suvasini)
varieties.origin: Karnataka (Coorg region)
varieties.traits: Malabar type, compact growth habit making it suitable for high-density planting (3000+ plants/ha)
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding in intensive management
varieties.availability: Karnataka State Spices Development Board, Coorg nurseries

varieties.name: SKP-14
varieties.origin: Karnataka selection
varieties.traits: Commercial variety for Karnataka

varieties.name: Appangala-2
varieties.origin: ICRI Cardamom Research Centre, Appangala, Karnataka
varieties.traits: Rhizome rot resistant variety, selection from open pollinated progeny of early bearing plants (4th generation of clone-37 from RRS Mudigere)
varieties.yield_notes: 238-848 kg/ha dry capsules
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to Katte/mosaic disease, rhizome rot resistant, less susceptible to foliar diseases

varieties.name: Mysore Vazhukka 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Karnataka-Kerala border regions
varieties.traits: Variant of Vazhukka type with Mysore characteristics

varieties.name: Sundhari 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala - Local selection
varieties.traits: Name means 'Beautiful' in Malayalam

varieties.name: Panikkulangara (Panikulangara) 🌱
varieties.origin: Kerala - Named after location
varieties.traits: Local geographic selection

varieties.name: Valley Green
varieties.origin: Kerala valley selections
varieties.traits: Developed for valley cultivation

varieties.name: White-Flowered Vazhukka
varieties.origin: Developed by K.J. Baby, Idukki district, Kerala
varieties.traits: Purely white-flowered variety (unique mutation), Vazhukka type, high adaptability to different shade conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yield than Njallani
varieties.availability: Limited - K.J. Baby's holdings, under evaluation by research institutes

varieties.name: CL-730
varieties.origin: Selection from germplasm collection
varieties.yield_notes: 1.57 kg green capsule per clump (vs 1.2 kg in Mudigere-1)
varieties.availability: Research stations - not yet commercially released

varieties.name: CL-692
varieties.origin: Selection from germplasm collection
varieties.yield_notes: 1.53 kg green capsule per clump

varieties.name: CL-D-237
varieties.origin: Clone developed from open-pollinated seedling progenies
varieties.yield_notes: 1.80 kg green capsule per clump (highest among experimental clones, vs 1.40 kg in Mudigere-2)

varieties.name: MCC-12, MCC-16, MCC-40, MCC-49
varieties.origin: ICRI selections from various collections (MCC = collection numbers)
varieties.traits: High-yielding clonal selections under evaluation

varieties.name: NCC-200, TDK-4, TDK-11
varieties.origin: Various research stations (NCC, TDK = research codes)
varieties.traits: High-yielding selections

varieties.name: P3 & P6 (Drought-Tolerant Lines)
varieties.origin: Karnataka research selection
varieties.traits: Drought tolerant/resistant characteristics (important for climate adaptation)

varieties.name: KE-2 (Katte-Tolerant)
varieties.origin: ICRI RRS Sakleshpur - Developed through mutation breeding
varieties.traits: Katte disease tolerant
varieties.availability: In pipeline for release from ICRI Sakleshpur

varieties.name: ICRI Accession 594
varieties.origin: ICRI - Developed 2021-22
varieties.traits: Developed through natural selection and genetic upgradation, desirable biotic and abiotic characters
varieties.yield_notes: 3000 kg/ha potential under moderate management

varieties.name: MHC-1 & MHC-2
varieties.origin: ICRI Myladumpara cardamom hybrids
varieties.traits: Notified by ICAR-NBPGR, assigned IC645599 and IC645600

varieties.name: Elite Clone MCC-549
varieties.origin: ICRI Biotechnology Division
varieties.traits: Notified as unique variety by ICAR-NBPGR, assigned IC645601 for National Identity
